endCard.title: End of the Introduction
endCard.subtitle: You've completed the introduction of Zero The Hero.

death.epitaph:
Nothing made sense,
it was all for nothing.

tutorial.move: Move in the 4 directions. Hold to keep walking.
tutorial.combat: Combat is automatic: walk toward an enemy to attack it.

lockedLines.speaker: npc
lockedLines.text: It's too dark... I can barely think.

lockedLines.speaker: narrator
lockedLines.text: The person stares at the dead fire, shivering.

lockedLines.speaker: npc
lockedLines.text: Light the fire. When this place is safe, we can talk.

intro.speaker: narrator
intro.text: The old wizard adjusts his hat. For a moment, his eyes meet yours.

intro.speaker: npc
intro.text: I have waited for this moment for ages.

intro.speaker: npc
intro.text: You have come from lands far away.

intro.speaker: npc
intro.text: You are Zero... the last heir of a forgotten crusade.

intro.speaker: npc
intro.text: But you are not a knight yet.

intro.speaker: npc
intro.text: You still have to become one.

protect.speaker: narrator
protect.text: The wind blows through the ruins.

protect.speaker: npc
protect.text: This is the last flame of the world.

protect.speaker: npc
protect.text: Beyond this flame... there is only darkness.

protect.speaker: npc
protect.text: A void where even hope has ceased to exist.

protect.speaker: npc
protect.text: As long as it stays lit... the world can still be reborn.

prophecy.speaker: npc
prophecy.text: You walk the path described by the ancients.

prophecy.speaker: npc
prophecy.text: The primordial flame awakens once more.

prophecy.speaker: npc
prophecy.text: What was only a spark now begins to light the world.

prophecy.speaker: npc
prophecy.text: The flame that will save this world is the same one that burns within you.

prophecy.speaker: npc
prophecy.text: Do not let it go out.

prophecy.speaker: npc
prophecy.text: You've played the brief introduction of Zero The Hero!

prophecy.speaker: npc
prophecy.text: Thank you so much for everything!

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You must be the hero who seeks to find the sword?

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Yes. I am a talking cat.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: And you're a naked primate in an armor.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Well, the game is still in the Alpha version, so I think it's impossible for you to find the sword.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Hello! I'm a completely normal NPC.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: He smiles in a way that doesn't look natural.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I'm not a mimic. That would be absurd.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: His eyes don't blink. Not once.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Please, keep walking.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: My ship crashed in this place, I'm trying to find its parts.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: But I think they landed on a part of the map that isn't available in the alpha version.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Work hard and you'll find the sword you're looking for.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I used an axe to cut uranium, but you can cut other things with it.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You must be the warrior Zero!

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I was on a journey to find the perfect place to do a painting.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: But these bushes are getting in the way.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You came at a good time. I've got offers that are irrelevant and insistent.

lines.speaker: narrator
lines.text: He opens his backpack, but there are only crumpled receipts.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Nothing in stock. But I can write down your interest.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Come back tomorrow. Or yesterday. My schedule is flexible.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You are not a hero.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Sorry for my manners, it's just that I'm sad.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I lost my inspiration to write poems.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Imagine a poet who doesn't write poems, I totally lost my identity.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Maybe one day I can get back to writing poems.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You know that writing poems doesn't make any money.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Can you make money being a hero?

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Ah, the chosen one. I felt your steps ripple through the weave of destiny.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: I could conjure the sword you seek... but my grimoire only compiles in the full version.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Go forth, hero. And beware the wizards who'd rather cast spells than talk.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Nihilist knight! Do you accept a game of chess for your soul?

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: You died, but you haven't reached the end of your journey.

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Thanks for playing the Alpha version of the game!

lines.speaker: npc
lines.text: Submit your feedback.
